# Phase 0 — Connect Prototype to the Real Report API Implementation Plan

> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.

**Goal:** Switch fieldsight-ui's read surfaces from in-memory mocks to the real, already-deployed report API (Cognito login + live data on the Amplify dev URL), while write surfaces that have no backend stay safely mocked.

**Architecture:** A per-environment `env.js` (generated by Amplify at build time, absent locally) seeds `window.FS_ENV`; `scripts/api/index.js` consumes it when initializing `FS.api`. A new `writeMocks` flag keeps backend-less write paths on mocks even when reads go live. The one write endpoint that DOES exist (action toggle) is fixed to match the real route and goes live. A session bridge mirrors the real Cognito user into `AuthMock.currentUser` so role-scoping code keeps working.

**Tech Stack:** In-browser React (no build step, Babel CDN), plain `<script>` IIFEs, Amplify static hosting, Cognito USER_PASSWORD_AUTH.

## Global Constraints

- **Real API base URL (must include the `/api` suffix — `_fetch.js:139-140` does bare `base + path` and module paths have NO `/api` prefix):**
  `https://khfj3p1fkb.execute-api.ap-southeast-2.amazonaws.com/prod/api`
- Cognito defaults in `scripts/auth/cognito.js:37-42` are ALREADY correct (pool `ap-southeast-2_ps7XIQGHB`, client `5npb81jbj1hgh9tsck25kan3os`) — do NOT duplicate them into env.js; env.js only needs `baseUrl`/`useMocks`.
- **No build step**: every changed loaded file needs its `?v=N` cache-buster bumped in `app-shell-preview.html`. Verify each changed JS file with `node --check <file>` before commit.
- Repo conventions in `fieldsight-ui/CLAUDE.md` are binding (IIFE modules on `window.FS`/`window.FieldSight`, hooks rules, token lockstep — not touched here).
- **Write endpoints that DO NOT exist** (BACKEND-CONTEXT.md §10): site/user management (`POST /sites`, `POST /users`, `PATCH /users/{id}`), safety/quality item creation, any upload. These must NOT fire real requests in this phase.
- **The action toggle endpoint DOES exist** but as `POST /api/actions/toggle` (BACKEND-CONTEXT.md §4.10), not the `PATCH /actions/{id}` the UI currently sends — fix the UI to the documented contract.
- Tokens stay in sessionStorage (never localStorage); presigned URLs are short-lived (never cached in localStorage).
- Work on branch `claude/phase0-real-data`; merge to `dev` only at the final deploy task (push to `dev` = Amplify auto-deploy).
- Git: CRLF warnings normal; NEVER `git add -A`; stage explicit paths.
- Verification is syntax-check + staged browser checks (repo has no test runner); the plan's final task includes the human-verified login smoke test.

---

## File Structure

```
scripts/env.example.js        # NEW — documented template; real env.js is generated per env
scripts/api/index.js          # MODIFY — consume window.FS_ENV; add writeMocks flag
scripts/api/sites.js          # MODIFY — write fns gate on writeMocks
scripts/api/actions.js        # MODIFY — toggle → POST /actions/toggle (live)
scripts/composites/safety-create-modal.js   # MODIFY — gate on writeMocks
scripts/composites/quality-create-modal.js  # MODIFY — gate on writeMocks
scripts/auth/session-bridge.js # NEW — mirror FS.session.user → AuthMock.currentUser
app-shell-preview.html        # MODIFY — <script src="env.js"> + bridge tag + mocks=1 escape + v-bumps
amplify.yml                   # MODIFY — emit dist/env.js from Amplify env vars
```

---

### Task 1: env seam — `FS_ENV` consumption + env.js loading + `?mocks=1` escape

**Files:**
- Modify: `scripts/api/index.js` (the `window.FS.api = {...}` init, near line 74)
- Create: `scripts/env.example.js`
- Modify: `app-shell-preview.html` (script tag + bootstrap block near lines 247-260)
- Modify: `amplify.yml` (generate `dist/env.js`)

**Interfaces:**
- Consumes: `window.FS_ENV` = `{ baseUrl?: string, useMocks?: boolean, writeMocks?: boolean }` (optional global, set by env.js before api scripts load).
- Produces: `FS.api.useMocks` / `FS.api.baseUrl` / `FS.api.writeMocks` initialized from `FS_ENV` with safe fallbacks (`true` / `'/api'` / `true`); query params `?mocks=0|1`, `?baseUrl=…`, `?writemocks=0|1` override at runtime.

- [ ] **Step 1: Create branch off dev**

```bash
git -C "C:/Users/camil/Dropbox/fieldsight-ui" checkout dev
git -C "C:/Users/camil/Dropbox/fieldsight-ui" pull --ff-only
git -C "C:/Users/camil/Dropbox/fieldsight-ui" checkout -b claude/phase0-real-data
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Modify `scripts/api/index.js` init to consume FS_ENV**

Replace the current object literal assignment:

```js
  window.FS.api = {
    useMocks: true,
    baseUrl: '/api',
```

with:

```js
  /* Per-environment config: env.js (generated at deploy time) sets
     window.FS_ENV before this script loads. Absent locally → mock mode. */
  var env = window.FS_ENV || {};

  window.FS.api = {
    useMocks: env.useMocks !== undefined ? !!env.useMocks : true,
    /* writeMocks: write paths WITHOUT a real backend stay mocked even when
       reads go live (Sprint-5 lesson: never ship writes without a backend). */
    writeMocks: env.writeMocks !== undefined ? !!env.writeMocks : true,
    baseUrl: env.baseUrl || '/api',
```

(keep every other property of the object exactly as-is).

- [ ] **Step 3: Create `scripts/env.example.js`**

```js
/* ==========================================================================
   env.js — per-environment config (TEMPLATE).
   --------------------------------------------------------------------------
   The REAL env.js is generated at deploy time (see amplify.yml) and is NOT
   committed. It must load BEFORE scripts/api/index.js. Absent → mock mode.
   NOTE: baseUrl MUST end with /api (module paths carry no /api prefix).
   ========================================================================== */
window.FS_ENV = {
  baseUrl: 'https://khfj3p1fkb.execute-api.ap-southeast-2.amazonaws.com/prod/api',
  useMocks: false,   // reads go to the real API
  writeMocks: true,  // backend-less writes stay mocked (Phase 3 flips this)
};
```

- [ ] **Step 4: Add the env.js script tag to `app-shell-preview.html`**

Immediately BEFORE the auth block (`<script src="scripts/auth/cognito.js?v=2">`), insert:

```html
  <!-- Per-environment config (generated at deploy; 404s locally → mock mode). -->
  <script src="env.js?v=1"></script>
```

- [ ] **Step 5: Extend the runtime-override bootstrap**

In the inline bootstrap block (near lines 247-260) that currently handles `baseUrl` and `mocks === '0'`, add symmetric escapes right after the existing two:

```js
      if (params.get('mocks') === '1' && window.FS && window.FS.api) { window.FS.api.useMocks = true; }
      var wm = params.get('writemocks');
      if (wm !== null && window.FS && window.FS.api) { window.FS.api.writeMocks = wm !== '0'; }
```

- [ ] **Step 6: amplify.yml — generate dist/env.js from Amplify env vars**

Add ONE build command after `cp app-shell-preview.html dist/index.html`:

```yaml
        - >-
          printf 'window.FS_ENV = { baseUrl: "%s", useMocks: %s, writeMocks: %s };\n'
          "${FS_BASEURL:-/api}" "${FS_USEMOCKS:-true}" "${FS_WRITEMOCKS:-true}"
          > dist/env.js
```

(Defaults keep any env-var-less build in mock mode — identical to today's behavior.)

- [ ] **Step 7: Verify**

```bash
node --check scripts/api/index.js
node --check scripts/env.example.js
```
Expected: no output (clean). Also confirm `app-shell-preview.html` still lists env.js BEFORE cognito.js and the bootstrap edits are inside the existing IIFE/script block.

- [ ] **Step 8: Commit**

```bash
git -C "C:/Users/camil/Dropbox/fieldsight-ui" add scripts/api/index.js scripts/env.example.js app-shell-preview.html amplify.yml
git -C "C:/Users/camil/Dropbox/fieldsight-ui" commit -m "feat(env): FS_ENV seam — deploy-time env.js drives baseUrl/useMocks/writeMocks"
```

### Task 2: write-path isolation + real action toggle

**Files:**
- Modify: `scripts/api/sites.js` (write fns near lines 64, 82, 89)
- Modify: `scripts/api/actions.js` (toggle near line 64; create near line 130)
- Modify: `scripts/composites/safety-create-modal.js` (real-POST branch near line 107)
- Modify: `scripts/composites/quality-create-modal.js` (real-POST branch near line 87)

**Interfaces:**
- Consumes: `FS.api.useMocks`, `FS.api.writeMocks` (Task 1).
- Produces: backend-less writes (`createSite`, `createUser`, `updateUserRole`, safety/quality create, `actions.create`) fire real requests ONLY when `useMocks === false && writeMocks === false`; `actions.toggle` goes live whenever `useMocks === false`, via `POST /actions/toggle`.

- [ ] **Step 1: Read the exact current guards**

Read `scripts/api/sites.js`, `scripts/api/actions.js`, and both modal files. Each write function opens with a real-branch guard of the form `if (!window.FS.api.useMocks) { … real fetch … }` (line refs above). Also read BACKEND-CONTEXT.md §4.10 for the exact toggle request/response contract.

- [ ] **Step 2: Gate backend-less writes on writeMocks**

In `sites.js` (`createSite`, `createUser`, `updateUserRole`), `actions.js` (the POST-create function only), `safety-create-modal.js`, `quality-create-modal.js`: change each real-branch guard from

```js
if (!window.FS.api.useMocks) {
```

to

```js
if (!window.FS.api.useMocks && !window.FS.api.writeMocks) {
```

so with `useMocks=false, writeMocks=true` these fall through to their existing mock bodies (session-scoped, as today). Do not touch read functions.

- [ ] **Step 3: Fix the action toggle to the real endpoint**

In `scripts/api/actions.js`, the toggle function's real branch currently issues `PATCH /actions/{id}` with `{done}`. Replace it to match BACKEND-CONTEXT §4.10: `POST /actions/toggle` with the documented body/response shape (read §4.10 and mirror it exactly — it is the contract the deployed Lambda's `toggle_action` implements). Keep the guard as plain `if (!window.FS.api.useMocks)` — this endpoint exists and SHOULD go live. Keep the mock fallback unchanged.

- [ ] **Step 4: Verify**

```bash
node --check scripts/api/sites.js
node --check scripts/api/actions.js
node --check scripts/composites/safety-create-modal.js
node --check scripts/composites/quality-create-modal.js
```
Expected: clean. Grep to confirm no OTHER write fn still fires on bare `!useMocks`:
`grep -n "useMocks" scripts/api/*.js scripts/composites/safety-create-modal.js scripts/composites/quality-create-modal.js`

- [ ] **Step 5: Commit**

```bash
git -C "C:/Users/camil/Dropbox/fieldsight-ui" add scripts/api/sites.js scripts/api/actions.js scripts/composites/safety-create-modal.js scripts/composites/quality-create-modal.js
git -C "C:/Users/camil/Dropbox/fieldsight-ui" commit -m "feat(api): writeMocks isolation for backend-less writes; action toggle → real POST /actions/toggle"
```

### Task 3: session bridge — real login populates AuthMock.currentUser

Many modules read `window.AuthMock.currentUser` directly (e.g. `timeline.js:38`, `actions.js:116`, `sites.js:20`) for role/name scoping. In live mode the signed-in identity lives in `FS.session` — without a bridge, those modules keep seeing the hardcoded mock persona.

**Files:**
- Create: `scripts/auth/session-bridge.js`
- Modify: `app-shell-preview.html` (script tag AFTER auth-mock.js and session.js)

**Interfaces:**
- Consumes: `FS.session` (read its actual getter API from `scripts/auth/session.js` — user shape `{ sub, email, name, role, display_name, sites }` per session.js:54) and `window.AuthMock` (read its shape/API from `scripts/auth-mock.js` — mock persona `{ name, role, initials, site, … }` plus any update/notify mechanism it exposes).
- Produces: whenever a real session is signed in (at boot restore AND after login/hydrateUser), `AuthMock.currentUser` mirrors the real user: `name` ← `display_name || name || email`, `role` ← `role`, `initials` derived from the name (first letters of first two words, uppercased), site fields ← from `sites` if the mock shape carries them. In mock mode (`useMocks !== false`) the bridge does nothing.

- [ ] **Step 1: Read the two source files**

Read `scripts/auth/session.js` and `scripts/auth-mock.js` fully. Identify: (a) session's public getters + any subscription/callback hook; (b) AuthMock's exact currentUser field set and whether it exposes an update function or listeners that must be invoked for pages to re-render.

- [ ] **Step 2: Implement `scripts/auth/session-bridge.js`**

An IIFE that: does nothing when `window.FS.api.useMocks !== false`; otherwise on (a) initial load after session restore and (b) sign-in completion (use whatever notification mechanism session.js/login flow exposes — if none, patch into `FS.session.set` by wrapping it), maps the real user onto `AuthMock.currentUser` using AuthMock's own update path (so existing listeners fire). Follow repo IIFE conventions ('use strict', attach nothing new to globals beyond the wrapper). Match the field mapping in the Interfaces block. If AuthMock's shape has fields with no real-session equivalent, leave the mock defaults in place rather than blanking them.

- [ ] **Step 3: Load it in `app-shell-preview.html`**

Add AFTER both `scripts/auth-mock.js` and `scripts/auth/session.js` tags (order matters — it references both):

```html
  <script src="scripts/auth/session-bridge.js?v=1"></script>
```

- [ ] **Step 4: Verify**

```bash
node --check scripts/auth/session-bridge.js
```
Expected: clean. Confirm tag order in the HTML: auth-mock.js … session.js … session-bridge.js.

- [ ] **Step 5: Commit**

```bash
git -C "C:/Users/camil/Dropbox/fieldsight-ui" add scripts/auth/session-bridge.js app-shell-preview.html
git -C "C:/Users/camil/Dropbox/fieldsight-ui" commit -m "feat(auth): bridge real Cognito session into AuthMock.currentUser for live mode"
```

---

### Task 4: cache-busters, deploy wiring, and the live smoke test

**Files:**
- Modify: `app-shell-preview.html` (bump `?v=N` for every JS file changed in Tasks 1-3)
- Amplify: set branch env vars + merge to dev (controller/human steps included)

- [ ] **Step 1: Bump cache-busters**

In `app-shell-preview.html`, bump the `?v=N` for: `scripts/api/index.js`, `scripts/api/sites.js`, `scripts/api/actions.js`, `scripts/composites/safety-create-modal.js`, `scripts/composites/quality-create-modal.js` (new files env.js/session-bridge.js already carry `?v=1`). Commit:

```bash
git -C "C:/Users/camil/Dropbox/fieldsight-ui" add app-shell-preview.html
git -C "C:/Users/camil/Dropbox/fieldsight-ui" commit -m "chore: cache-buster bumps for Phase 0 files"
```

- [ ] **Step 2 (controller): set Amplify env vars on the dev branch**

```bash
aws amplify update-branch --app-id d2fssznicvuckr --branch-name dev --region ap-southeast-2 \
  --environment-variables FS_BASEURL=https://khfj3p1fkb.execute-api.ap-southeast-2.amazonaws.com/prod/api,FS_USEMOCKS=false,FS_WRITEMOCKS=true
```

- [ ] **Step 3 (controller): merge to dev and push (triggers Amplify build)**

```bash
git -C "C:/Users/camil/Dropbox/fieldsight-ui" checkout dev
git -C "C:/Users/camil/Dropbox/fieldsight-ui" merge claude/phase0-real-data
git -C "C:/Users/camil/Dropbox/fieldsight-ui" push
```

- [ ] **Step 4 (controller): verify the Amplify build emitted env.js**

Wait for the Amplify job to succeed (`aws amplify list-jobs --app-id d2fssznicvuckr --branch-name dev --max-items 1`), then `curl -s https://dev.d2fssznicvuckr.amplifyapp.com/env.js` — expect the FS_ENV line with the real baseUrl and `useMocks: false`.

- [ ] **Step 5 (human): login smoke test on the dev URL**

Open https://dev.d2fssznicvuckr.amplifyapp.com (Ctrl+Shift+R). Expected: LOGIN SCREEN (not the mock shell). Sign in with a real Cognito user (first login → NEW_PASSWORD_REQUIRED flow). Then verify: sites list is real; calendar dates = real report dates; timeline shows real topics; transcripts/audio/video load via presigned URLs; Ask answers against the selected report; action checkbox toggle persists after reload (real POST /actions/toggle); Safety/Quality "+ Log Item" still behaves as before (mock, session-scoped); `?mocks=1` escape returns the full mock demo.

**验证:** all read surfaces show S3-backed data for the signed-in role; nothing fires requests to nonexistent endpoints (Network tab shows no 4xx/5xx except the documented CloudFront-style 404-to-JSON handling); write modals unchanged.

**取舍:** Phase A/B/C write features stay mock-backed by design (Phase 3 gives them a real backend and flips `writeMocks`).
